name: Crunchy Snacks for Kids
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare a mixture of crackers using less sugar and low salt variety, or fill the cookie jar with peanuts-in-shell or roasted unsalted nuts. You can also keep a jar of mixed nuts, raisins, and chocolate chips (often referred to as "gorp").
2. For popcorn treats, sprinkle grated Parmesan cheese or American cheese on popped popcorn. Alternatively, mix it with peanuts or cinnamon.
3. Package extra popcorn in small plastic bags for convenient outdoor snacks, car rides, or TV specials (lunch boxes).
4. Create honey "cracker jacks" by heating honey and margarine until blended, then cooling the mixture. Pour over mixed popcorn and peanuts, stirring as you pour. Spread the coated popcorn in a single layer on a baking sheet and bake at 350°F for 5-10 minutes or until crisp, stirring several times.
5. Package the cooled crackers in plastic bags with twist ties for easy storage.
6. Make popcorn candy clusters by melting semi-sweet chocolate chips. Add popped corn and nuts, stirring until well coated. Drop spoonfuls onto a cookie sheet or wax paper and let set firm. For quicker consumption, chill the clusters in the freezer for 5 minutes before storing them in plastic bags.
7. Prepare a crunchy mix of cereal (such as Cheerios or Corn Chex), peanuts or mixed nuts, pretzel sticks, and seasoned croutons. In a shallow pan, combine 1/2 cup salad oil or 6 tbsp melted butter with 2 tsp Worcestershire sauce and 1/4 tsp garlic powder. Coat the crunchy ingredients evenly.
8. Place the coated mix in an oven preheated to 250°F and bake for about 45 minutes, stirring every 15 minutes. Allow the mixture to cool on absorbent paper before serving.
